1.text: Now I, Moroni, will give an account of those ancient people who were destroyed by the Lord in this northern land.
I am taking my account from the twenty-four gold plates that were found by the people of Limhi, which is called the Book of Ether.

I believe that the first part of this record, which talks about the creation of the world, and also about Adam, and everything from that time up to the great tower, and whatever happened among people until that time, is already contained in the records of the Jews.
So I am not writing about the things that happened from Adam's time until then; but those things are on the plates, and whoever finds them will be able to get the full account.
But I am not giving the full account—only part of it, from the tower until they were destroyed.
And this is how I give the account. The one who wrote this record was Ether, and he was a descendant of Coriantor.
Coriantor was the son of Moron.
And Moron was the son of Ethem.
And Ethem was the son of Ahah.
And Ahah was the son of Seth.
And Seth was the son of Shiblon.
And Shiblon was the son of Com.
And Com was the son of Coriantum.
And Coriantum was the son of Amnigaddah.
And Amnigaddah was the son of Aaron.
And Aaron was a descendant of Heth, who was the son of Hearthom.
And Hearthom was the son of Lib.
And Lib was the son of Kish.
And Kish was the son of Corom.
And Corom was the son of Levi.
And Levi was the son of Kim.
And Kim was the son of Morianton.
And Morianton was a descendant of Riplakish.
And Riplakish was the son of Shez.
And Shez was the son of Heth.
And Heth was the son of Com.
And Com was the son of Coriantum.
And Coriantum was the son of Emer.
And Emer was the son of Omer.
And Omer was the son of Shule.
And Shule was the son of Kib.
And Kib was the son of Orihah, who was the son of Jared.

This Jared came from the great tower with his brother and their families, along with some others and their families, at the time the Lord confused the language of the people and declared in his anger that they would be scattered across the whole earth. And according to the Lord's word, the people were scattered.

The brother of Jared was a large and mighty man who was greatly favored by the Lord. Jared said to his brother: Pray to the Lord so that he will not confuse our language and make it so we cannot understand each other.
So the brother of Jared prayed to the Lord, and the Lord had compassion on Jared. He did not confuse the language of Jared, and Jared and his brother could still understand each other.
Then Jared said to his brother: Pray again to the Lord, and perhaps he will turn away his anger from our friends so that their language is not confused either.
And the brother of Jared prayed to the Lord, and the Lord had compassion on their friends and their families also, so their language was not confused.

Then Jared spoke again to his brother, saying: Go and ask the Lord whether he will drive us out of the land, and if he will, ask him where we should go. Who knows—perhaps the Lord will lead us to a land that is the best of all the earth? And if so, let us be faithful to the Lord, so that we may receive it as our inheritance.
And the brother of Jared prayed to the Lord according to what Jared had asked.
And the Lord heard the brother of Jared and had compassion on him, and said to him:

Go and gather together your flocks, both male and female of every kind, and also seeds of every kind from the earth, and your families, and also your brother Jared and his family, and also your friends and their families, and the friends of Jared and their families.
And when you have done this, you shall lead them down into the valley to the north. I will meet you there, and I will go before you into a land that is the best of all the lands of the earth.

And there I will bless you and your descendants, and I will raise up from your descendants, and from the descendants of your brother, and from those who go with you, a great nation. There will be no nation greater than the one I will raise up from your descendants upon the whole earth. And I will do this for you because you have prayed to me for such a long time.

2.text: Jared and his brother, along with their families and the friends of Jared and his brother and their families, went down into the valley to the north. (The valley was called Nimrod, named after the mighty hunter.) They brought their flocks that they had gathered together, male and female of every kind.
They also set snares and caught birds, and they prepared a container in which they carried fish from the waters.
They also carried with them deseret, which means a honey bee. So they carried swarms of bees with them, and all kinds of things from the land, including seeds of every kind.
When they had come down into the valley of Nimrod, the Lord came down and spoke with the brother of Jared. He was in a cloud, and the brother of Jared could not see him.

The Lord commanded them to travel into the wilderness, into an area where no person had ever been. The Lord went before them and spoke with them as he stood in a cloud, giving them directions on where to travel.
They traveled through the wilderness and built barges, in which they crossed many bodies of water, being guided continually by the hand of the Lord.

The Lord would not allow them to stop beyond the sea in the wilderness but wanted them to continue all the way to the promised land, which was the best of all lands—a land the Lord God had preserved for a righteous people.

And the Lord had declared solemnly to the brother of Jared that whoever would possess this promised land, from that time forward and forever, must serve him, the true and only God, or they would be swept away when the fullness of his anger came upon them.

Now we can see God's decrees concerning this land: it is a promised land, and whatever nation possesses it must serve God, or they will be swept away when the fullness of his anger comes upon them. And the fullness of his anger comes when they are fully ripened in wickedness.

For this is a land that is the best above all other lands. Whoever possesses it must serve God or will be swept away, for this is the everlasting decree of God. It is not until the people of the land are fully ripened in wickedness that they are swept away.

And this message comes to you, O Gentiles, so that you may know the decrees of God—that you may repent and not continue in your sins until the fullness comes, and so you may not bring down the fullness of God's anger upon you as the previous inhabitants of the land have done.

This is a choice land, and whatever nation possesses it will be free from bondage, captivity, and domination by all other nations, if they will only serve the God of the land, who is Jesus Christ, who has been revealed by the things we have written.

Now I continue with my record. The Lord brought Jared and his people all the way to that great sea that divides the lands. When they came to the sea, they set up their tents. They named the place Moriancumer, and they lived in tents on the seashore for four years.

At the end of four years, the Lord came again to the brother of Jared and stood in a cloud and spoke with him. For three hours the Lord spoke with the brother of Jared and rebuked him because he had not remembered to pray to the Lord.

The brother of Jared repented of the wrong he had done and prayed to the Lord for his people who were with him. The Lord said to him: I will forgive you and your people of their sins; but you must not sin anymore, for you must remember that my Spirit will not always work with people. If you continue to sin until you are fully ripe in wickedness, you will be cut off from the Lord's presence. These are my intentions for the land I will give you as your inheritance, for it will be a land that is the best above all other lands.

And the Lord said: Go to work and build barges the same way you built them before. So the brother of Jared and his people went to work and built barges the same way they had built them before, following the Lord's instructions. The barges were small and light on the water, as light as a bird on the water.

They were built so that they were extremely watertight—as tight as a dish. The bottom was tight like a dish, the sides were tight like a dish, the ends were pointed, and the top was tight like a dish. They were about the length of a tree. And when the door was shut, it was tight like a dish.
The brother of Jared prayed to the Lord, saying: O Lord, I have done the work you commanded me, and I have built the barges just as you directed.
But Lord, there is no light in them. How will we steer? And we will die, because we cannot breathe in them except for the air that is already inside. We will suffocate.

The Lord said to the brother of Jared: You shall make a hole in the top and also in the bottom. When you need air, open the hole and let air in. If water starts coming in, close the hole so you do not drown.
And the brother of Jared did as the Lord had commanded.

And he prayed again to the Lord, saying: O Lord, I have done everything you commanded me. I have prepared the vessels for my people, but there is no light in them. O Lord, will you really have us cross this great body of water in darkness?

And the Lord said to the brother of Jared: What would you like me to do so you can have light in your vessels? You cannot have windows because they would be shattered. And you cannot take fire with you, because you will not travel by the light of fire.

For you will be like a whale in the middle of the sea, because mountain-sized waves will crash upon you. But I will bring you up again out of the depths of the sea, for the winds have gone out from my mouth, and I have also sent the rains and the floods.

I am preparing you to face these things, for you cannot cross this great deep water unless I prepare you to withstand the waves of the sea, the winds, and the floods that will come. So what would you like me to prepare for you so that you may have light when you are swallowed up in the depths of the sea?

3.text: The brother of Jared (now there were eight vessels that had been prepared) went up to a mountain they called Mount Shelem, because of its great height. He melted from the rock sixteen small stones. They were white and clear, as transparent as glass. He carried them in his hands to the top of the mountain and prayed again to the Lord, saying:

O Lord, you have said that we must be surrounded by the floods. Now please, O Lord, do not be angry with your servant because of his weakness before you. We know that you are holy and dwell in the heavens, and that we are unworthy before you. Because of the fall, our nature has become continually evil. Nevertheless, O Lord, you have given us a commandment to call upon you, so that we may receive from you according to our desires.

O Lord, you have punished us because of our sins and have driven us out, and for many years we have been in the wilderness. Yet you have been merciful to us. O Lord, look upon me with compassion and turn away your anger from your people, and do not let them cross this fierce deep water in darkness. Please look at these things I have melted out of the rock.

And I know, O Lord, that you have all power and can do whatever you will for the good of mankind. Therefore, touch these stones, O Lord, with your finger and prepare them so they will shine in the darkness. They will give us light in the vessels we have prepared, so that we may have light while we cross the sea.
O Lord, you can do this. We know that you are able to show great power, even power that seems small to the understanding of men.

When the brother of Jared had said these words, the Lord stretched out his hand and touched the stones one by one with his finger. And the veil was taken from the eyes of the brother of Jared, and he saw the finger of the Lord. It looked like the finger of a man, like flesh and blood. And the brother of Jared fell down before the Lord, because he was struck with fear.
The Lord saw that the brother of Jared had fallen to the ground, and the Lord said to him: Stand up. Why have you fallen?
And he said to the Lord: I saw the finger of the Lord, and I was afraid he would strike me. I did not know that the Lord had flesh and blood.

And the Lord said to him: Because of your faith you have seen that I will take upon myself flesh and blood. Never has anyone come before me with such great faith as you have. If it were not so, you could not have seen my finger. Did you see anything more than this?
And he answered: No. Lord, show yourself to me.
And the Lord said to him: Do you believe the words that I will speak?
And he answered: Yes, Lord, I know that you speak the truth, for you are a God of truth and cannot lie.

And when he had said these words, the Lord showed himself to him and said: Because you know these things, you are redeemed from the fall. Therefore you are brought back into my presence, and that is why I show myself to you.

I am the one who was prepared from the foundation of the world to redeem my people. I am Jesus Christ. I am the Father and the Son. In me all mankind will have life, and that eternally—even all who believe on my name. And they will become my sons and my daughters.

Never before have I shown myself to anyone I have created, for never has anyone believed in me as you have. Do you see that you are created in my own image? Yes, all people were created in the beginning in my own image.
This body that you now see is the body of my spirit. I created mankind after the body of my spirit. And just as I appear to you now in the spirit, so will I appear to my people in the flesh.

Now, as I, Moroni, said, I could not write a full account of these things, so it is enough for me to say that Jesus showed himself to this man in the spirit, in the same form and likeness of the body in which he later showed himself to the Nephites.
And he ministered to him just as he ministered to the Nephites. All of this happened so that this man would know that he was God, because of the many great works the Lord had shown him.

And because of this man's knowledge, he could not be kept from seeing beyond the veil. He saw the finger of Jesus, and when he saw it, he fell with fear, for he knew it was the finger of the Lord. He no longer had mere faith, for he knew with certainty, without any doubt.
Therefore, having this perfect knowledge of God, he could not be kept from seeing beyond the veil. So he saw Jesus, and Jesus ministered to him.

And the Lord said to the brother of Jared: You must not allow these things that you have seen and heard to be shared with the world until the time comes when I will glorify my name in the flesh. Therefore, treasure up the things you have seen and heard, and show them to no one.
And when you come unto me, you shall write them down and seal them up so that no one can interpret them, for you will write them in a language that cannot be read.
And I will give you these two stones, and you shall seal them up along with the things you write.
For I have confused the language in which you will write. Therefore, in my own due time, I will cause these stones to make clear to the eyes of men the things you will write.

And when the Lord had said these words, he showed the brother of Jared all the inhabitants of the earth who had ever lived, and also all who would yet live. He withheld nothing from his sight, even to the ends of the earth.

For the Lord had told him before that if he would believe in him, he could show him all things—and it would be shown to him. Therefore the Lord could not withhold anything from him, because he knew that the Lord could show him all things.
And the Lord said to him: Write these things and seal them up, and I will show them in my own due time to the children of men.
And the Lord commanded him to seal up the two stones he had received and not show them until the Lord would reveal them to the children of men.

4.text: The Lord commanded the brother of Jared to go down from the mountain, away from the Lord's presence, and write the things he had seen. These writings were not to come to the people until after Christ would be lifted up on the cross. For this reason, King Mosiah kept them, so they would not come to the world until after Christ had shown himself to his people.
And after Christ had truly shown himself to his people, he commanded that these writings should be made known.

But now, after that, everyone has fallen into unbelief. There is no one left except the Lamanites, and they have rejected the gospel of Christ. Therefore I am commanded to hide these things up again in the earth.
I have written on these plates the very things the brother of Jared saw, and never were greater things revealed than what was revealed to the brother of Jared.

Therefore the Lord commanded me to write them, and I have written them. He commanded me to seal them up, and he also commanded me to seal up the interpretation of them. So I have sealed up the interpreters, according to the Lord's commandment.
For the Lord said to me: They shall not go to the Gentiles until the day they repent of their wickedness and become clean before the Lord.

And in that day when they exercise faith in me, says the Lord, even as the brother of Jared did, so they may become sanctified in me—then I will reveal to them the things the brother of Jared saw, even unfolding to them all my revelations, says Jesus Christ, the Son of God, the Father of the heavens and of the earth and all things in them.

And whoever fights against the word of the Lord, let him be cursed; and whoever denies these things, let him be cursed. For I will show them no greater things, says Jesus Christ, for I am the one who speaks.
At my command the heavens are opened and shut. At my word the earth shall shake. At my command the inhabitants of the earth shall pass away, as if by fire.
And whoever does not believe my words does not believe my disciples. And if I do not speak, then judge for yourselves, for you will know that it is I who speaks at the last day.

But whoever believes these things I have spoken, I will visit with the manifestations of my Spirit, and that person shall know and testify. Because of my Spirit, that person shall know that these things are true, for it persuades people to do good.

And whatever persuades people to do good comes from me, for good comes from no one except me. I am the one who leads people to all good. Whoever will not believe my words will not believe me—that I exist. And whoever will not believe me will not believe the Father who sent me. For I am the Father, I am the light, the life, and the truth of the world.
Come unto me, O Gentiles, and I will show you the greater things—the knowledge that is hidden because of unbelief.

Come unto me, O house of Israel, and it will be revealed to you how great are the things the Father has stored up for you from the foundation of the world—things that have not come to you because of unbelief.

When you tear away that veil of unbelief that causes you to remain in your terrible state of wickedness, hardness of heart, and blindness of mind—then the great and marvelous things that have been hidden from you since the foundation of the world will be revealed. Yes, when you call upon the Father in my name with a broken heart and a humble spirit, then you will know that the Father has remembered the covenant he made with your ancestors, O house of Israel.
And then the revelations that I had my servant John write will be unfolded before all people. Remember, when you see these things, you will know that the time is near for them to be made fully known.
Therefore, when you receive this record, you may know that the Father's work has begun across the whole earth.

Therefore, repent, all you people of the earth, and come unto me, and believe in my gospel, and be baptized in my name. For whoever believes and is baptized will be saved, but whoever does not believe will be condemned. And signs will follow those who believe in my name.

And blessed is the one who is found faithful to my name at the last day, for that person will be lifted up to live in the kingdom prepared for him from the foundation of the world. And it is I who have spoken it. Amen.

5.text: And now I, Moroni, have written the words I was commanded to write, according to my memory. I have told you about the things I have sealed up. Therefore, do not touch them so that you may translate them, for that is forbidden to you, unless in time God considers it wise.
And you may be permitted to show the plates to those who will help bring this work forward.
And the plates will be shown to three people by the power of God, and they will know with certainty that these things are true.

And by the testimony of three witnesses, these things will be established. The testimony of three, along with this work, will show the power of God and also his word, of which the Father, the Son, and the Holy Ghost bear record. And all of this will stand as a testimony against the world at the last day.
And if people repent and come to the Father in the name of Jesus, they will be received into the kingdom of God.
And now, if I have no authority for these things, judge for yourselves. You will know that I have authority when you see me, and we will stand before God at the last day. Amen.

6.text: And now I, Moroni, continue with the record of Jared and his brother.

After the Lord had prepared the stones that the brother of Jared had carried up the mountain, the brother of Jared came down from the mountain and placed the stones into the vessels that had been prepared, one in each end. And the stones gave light to the vessels.
And so the Lord caused stones to shine in darkness, to give light to men, women, and children, so they would not have to cross the great waters in darkness.

When they had prepared all kinds of food so they could survive on the water, along with food for their flocks and herds and whatever animals or birds they were bringing with them—when they had done all these things, they boarded their vessels and set out into the sea, entrusting themselves to the Lord their God.
And the Lord God caused a powerful wind to blow across the waters toward the promised land, and so they were tossed upon the waves of the sea by the wind.
They were many times buried in the depths of the sea because of the enormous waves that crashed upon them and the great and terrible storms caused by the fierce wind.

And when they were buried in the deep, no water could hurt them because their vessels were watertight like a dish, and also tight like the ark of Noah. So when they were surrounded by water, they prayed to the Lord, and he brought them back up to the surface.
The wind never stopped blowing toward the promised land while they were on the water, and so they were driven forward by the wind.
And they sang praises to the Lord. The brother of Jared sang praises to the Lord, and he thanked and praised the Lord all day long. And when night came, they still did not stop praising the Lord.
And so they were driven forward. No sea creature could break them apart, and no whale could damage them. They had light continually, whether they were above the water or below it.
And so they were driven forward for three hundred and forty-four days on the water.

And they landed on the shore of the promised land. When they set their feet on the shores of the promised land, they bowed down to the ground and humbled themselves before the Lord, and shed tears of joy before the Lord because of his many tender mercies toward them.
And they went across the land and began to farm the earth.
Jared had four sons, and their names were Jacom, Gilgah, Mahah, and Orihah.
And the brother of Jared also had sons and daughters.
The friends of Jared and his brother numbered about twenty-two people, and they also had sons and daughters before they came to the promised land. So they began to become a large group.
And they were taught to walk humbly before the Lord, and they were also taught from on high.
And they began to spread across the land, and to multiply, and to farm the earth, and they grew strong in the land.
The brother of Jared began to grow old and knew he would soon die. So he said to Jared: Let us gather our people together so we can count them and find out what they want from us before we die.
So the people were gathered together. Now the brother of Jared had twenty-two sons and daughters, and Jared had twelve children, having four sons.
After they had counted the people, they asked them what they would want them to do before they died.
And the people asked them to anoint one of their sons to be a king over them.
This was upsetting to them. And the brother of Jared said to them: Surely this will lead to captivity.
But Jared said to his brother: Let them have a king. So he told the people: Choose a king from among our sons—whichever one you want.

And they chose the firstborn son of the brother of Jared, whose name was Pagag. But he refused and would not be their king. The people wanted his father to force him, but his father would not, and he commanded them not to force anyone to be king.
Then they chose all the other brothers of Pagag, but none of them would do it either.
And none of Jared's sons would either, except for one. Orihah was anointed to be king over the people.
And he began to reign, and the people began to prosper. They became very wealthy.
And Jared died, and his brother also died.

And Orihah walked humbly before the Lord, and remembered how great the things were that the Lord had done for his father. He also taught his people how great the things were that the Lord had done for their fathers.

7.text: Orihah ruled the land with justice and righteousness all his days, and his days were very many.
He had sons and daughters—thirty-one children in all, including twenty-three sons.
In his old age he also had a son named Kib. And Kib reigned after him, and Kib had a son named Corihor.

When Corihor was thirty-two years old, he rebelled against his father and went to live in the land of Nehor. He had sons and daughters, and they became very attractive, so Corihor drew many people to follow him.

When he had gathered an army, he went up to the land of Moron, where the king lived, and took him prisoner. This fulfilled what the brother of Jared had said—that having a king would lead them into captivity.
Now the land of Moron, where the king lived, was near the land the Nephites called Desolation.
Kib lived in captivity, along with his people under Corihor his son, until Kib became very old. Nevertheless, Kib had a son named Shule while he was still in captivity, even in his old age.
Shule was angry with his brother. Shule grew strong and became as powerful as a man could be, and he was also wise in judgment.

So he went to the hill Ephraim and smelted ore from the hill and made swords of steel for those who had followed him. After he had armed them with swords, he returned to the city of Nehor and fought his brother Corihor. By this he won the kingdom and gave it back to his father Kib.
Because of what Shule had done, his father gave him the kingdom. So Shule began to reign in place of his father.
He ruled with justice and righteousness, and he spread his kingdom across the whole land, for the people had become very numerous.
And Shule also had many sons and daughters.
Corihor repented of the many wrongs he had done, so Shule gave him authority in his kingdom.
And Corihor had many sons and daughters. Among his sons was one named Noah.
Noah rebelled against King Shule and also against his father Corihor, and he persuaded his brother Cohor and all his other brothers and many of the people to follow him.
He fought against King Shule and won control of the land of their first settlement, and he became king over that part of the land.
He fought again against King Shule, and he captured King Shule and carried him away as a prisoner to Moron.

As he was about to put Shule to death, the sons of Shule sneaked into Noah's house at night and killed him. They broke down the prison door and brought their father out, and they placed him back on his throne in his own kingdom.
Therefore, Noah's son built up his kingdom in his place. However, they could not gain power over King Shule anymore, and the people under King Shule prospered greatly and grew strong.
The country was divided, and there were two kingdoms: the kingdom of Shule and the kingdom of Cohor, the son of Noah.
Cohor, the son of Noah, sent his people to fight against Shule, but Shule defeated them and killed Cohor.

Now Cohor had a son named Nimrod. Nimrod gave up the kingdom of Cohor to Shule, and he gained Shule's favor. So Shule gave him great privileges, and Nimrod was allowed to do as he wished in the kingdom of Shule.

Also during the reign of Shule, prophets came among the people, sent by the Lord, warning that the wickedness and idol worship of the people was bringing a curse upon the land and that they would be destroyed if they did not repent.
But the people spoke against the prophets and mocked them. King Shule punished everyone who spoke against the prophets.
He made a law throughout the whole land that gave the prophets the right to go wherever they wanted. Because of this, the people were brought to repentance.
Because the people repented of their wickedness and idol worship, the Lord spared them, and they began to prosper again in the land. And Shule had sons and daughters in his old age.

There were no more wars during the days of Shule. He remembered the great things the Lord had done for his ancestors in bringing them across the great deep water to the promised land, and he ruled with justice and righteousness all his days.

8.text: Shule had a son named Omer, and Omer reigned after him. Omer had a son named Jared, and Jared had sons and daughters.
Jared rebelled against his father and went to live in the land of Heth. He flattered many people with his clever words until he had gained half of the kingdom.
When he had gained half the kingdom, he attacked his father and took him prisoner and made him serve in captivity.
Now, during the reign of Omer, he was in captivity for half of his days. While in captivity, he had sons and daughters, among whom were Esrom and Coriantumr.
Esrom and Coriantumr were extremely angry because of what their brother Jared had done, so they raised an army and fought against Jared. They attacked him at night.
When they had defeated Jared's army, they were about to kill him too. But he begged them not to kill him and said he would give up the kingdom to his father. So they spared his life.
Now Jared became extremely sad because he had lost the kingdom, for he had set his heart on the kingdom and on worldly glory.
Now Jared's daughter was very clever. Seeing her father's sorrow, she thought of a plan to get the kingdom back for her father.

Jared's daughter was very beautiful. She talked with her father and said to him: Why is my father so full of sorrow? Hasn't he read the record that our ancestors brought across the great sea? Isn't there an account of ancient people who used their secret plans to gain kingdoms and great glory?

So let my father send for Akish, the son of Kimnor. I am beautiful, and I will dance before him and please him so that he will want me as his wife. When he asks you to give me to him in marriage, you shall say: I will give her to you if you bring me the head of my father, the king.
Now Omer was a friend of Akish. So when Jared sent for Akish, Jared's daughter danced before Akish and pleased him so much that he wanted her as his wife. He said to Jared: Give her to me as my wife.
And Jared said to him: I will give her to you if you bring me the head of my father, the king.
And Akish gathered all his relatives into Jared's house and said to them: Will you swear to me that you will be loyal to me in the thing I will ask of you?

And they all swore to him by the God of heaven, and also by the heavens, the earth, and their own lives, that whoever failed to give Akish the help he wanted would lose his head, and whoever revealed anything Akish told them would lose his life.

And so they agreed with Akish. Akish gave them the oaths that had been used by people of ancient times who also sought power—oaths that had been handed down from Cain, who was a murderer from the beginning.

These oaths were kept alive by the power of the devil, meant to keep people in darkness, to help those who sought power to gain it, and to murder, rob, lie, and commit all kinds of wickedness and immoral acts.

It was Jared's daughter who put the idea in his mind to search for these ancient practices. And Jared put it in the heart of Akish, and Akish spread it to his relatives and friends, leading them astray with attractive promises to do whatever he wanted.
And they formed a secret combination, just as the ancients had done. This kind of combination is the most wicked and evil of all, in the sight of God.
For the Lord does not work in secret combinations, nor does he want people to shed blood. He has forbidden it in all things, from the beginning of mankind.
And now I, Moroni, do not write the details of their oaths and combinations, for it has been made known to me that they exist among all people, including among the Lamanites.
And these secret combinations have caused the destruction of the people I am now writing about, and also the destruction of the people of Nephi.

And whatever nation supports such secret combinations in order to gain power and wealth, allowing them to spread across the nation—that nation will be destroyed. For the Lord will not allow the blood of his saints, which is shed by these people, to always cry to him from the ground for vengeance without him avenging them.

Therefore, O Gentiles, God in his wisdom has chosen to show you these things so that you may repent of your sins and not allow these murderous combinations to gain power over you—combinations that are built to get power and wealth. Otherwise, the work of destruction will come upon you. Yes, the sword of the justice of the Eternal God will fall upon you, bringing your overthrow and destruction, if you allow these things to continue.

Therefore, the Lord commands you: when you see these things happening among you, wake up and realize how terrible your situation is because of this secret combination that will be among you. Otherwise, woe to you because of the blood of those who have been killed, for they cry from the dust for vengeance upon those who built it up.

For whoever builds up these combinations seeks to overthrow the freedom of all lands, nations, and countries. It brings about the destruction of all people, for it is built up by the devil, who is the father of all lies—the same liar who deceived our first parents, the same liar who has caused people to commit murder from the beginning, who has hardened people's hearts so that they have murdered the prophets, stoned them, and driven them out from the beginning.

Therefore, I, Moroni, am commanded to write these things so that evil may be done away with, and so that the time may come when Satan will have no power over the hearts of the people, but that they may be persuaded to do good continually, so they may come to the fountain of all righteousness and be saved.

9.text: Now I, Moroni, continue with my record. Because of the secret alliances of Akish and his friends, they overthrew the kingdom of Omer.
However, the Lord was merciful to Omer, and also to his sons and daughters who did not seek to destroy him.

The Lord warned Omer in a dream to leave the land. So Omer left with his family and traveled many days. He passed by the hill of Shim and by the place where the Nephites were destroyed, then went eastward to a place called Ablom, by the seashore. There he set up camp with his sons, daughters, and all his household, except for Jared and his family.
Jared was made king over the people through wickedness, and he gave his daughter to Akish as a wife.
Akish then tried to kill his father-in-law. He called on those who had sworn the ancient oath, and they killed his father-in-law while he sat on his throne, meeting with his people.
This wicked and secret society had spread so much that it had corrupted the hearts of all the people. So Jared was murdered on his throne, and Akish ruled in his place.
Akish became jealous of his own son, so he locked him in prison and gave him little or no food until he died.
Now the brother of the one who died (whose name was Nimrah) was angry with his father for what he had done to his brother.
Nimrah gathered a small group of men and fled the land, and went to live with Omer.
Akish had other sons, and they won over the hearts of the people, even though the people had sworn to do whatever wicked thing Akish wanted.
The people of Akish wanted wealth, just as Akish wanted power. So the sons of Akish offered the people money, and by doing this they drew most of the people to follow them.

A war broke out between the sons of Akish and Akish, and it lasted for many years. It destroyed nearly all the people of the kingdom—all except thirty people, plus those who had fled with Omer's household.
Because of this, Omer was restored to the land of his inheritance.
Omer grew old, but in his old age he had a son named Emer. He anointed Emer to be king in his place.

After he had anointed Emer as king, Omer lived two more years of peace before he died, having lived a very long life full of sorrow. Emer then ruled in his place and followed in his father's footsteps.
The Lord began to remove the curse from the land, and Emer's household prospered greatly during his reign. Over sixty-two years they became very strong and very wealthy—
They had all kinds of fruit, grain, silks, fine linen, gold, silver, and precious things.
They also had all kinds of cattle—oxen, cows, sheep, pigs, and goats—and many other animals that were useful for food.
They also had horses, donkeys, and elephants, as well as cureloms and cumoms. All of these were useful, especially the elephants, cureloms, and cumoms.

The Lord poured out his blessings on this land, which was the most favored of all lands. He commanded that whoever lived in this land should serve the Lord, or they would be destroyed when their wickedness was fully ripe. For the Lord said: I will pour out the fullness of my anger upon such people.
Emer ruled with righteousness all his life. He had many sons and daughters, and he had a son named Coriantum, whom he anointed to rule in his place.
After anointing Coriantum, Emer lived four more years in peace. He even saw the Son of Righteousness and rejoiced in that day. He died in peace.
Coriantum followed in his father's footsteps. He built many great cities and did good for his people throughout his life. But he had no children until he was very old.
His wife died at the age of one hundred and two. Then Coriantum married a young woman in his old age and had sons and daughters. He lived to be one hundred and forty-two years old.
He had a son named Com, who ruled in his place for forty-nine years. Com had a son named Heth, and also other sons and daughters.
The people had spread across the whole land again, and great wickedness returned. Heth began to follow the secret plans of old in order to destroy his father.
Heth overthrew his father by killing him with his own sword, and then he ruled in his place.

Prophets came to the land again, calling the people to repentance. They warned that the people must prepare the way of the Lord, or a curse would come upon the land—a terrible famine that would destroy them if they did not repent.
But the people did not believe the prophets. They threw them out, and some they threw into pits and left to die. They did all of this at the command of King Heth.
A severe drought came upon the land, and the people began dying quickly because of it, for there was no rain at all.
Poisonous snakes also appeared throughout the land and killed many people. The people's flocks began fleeing from the snakes toward the land to the south, which the Nephites later called Zarahemla.
Many of the animals died along the way, but some made it to the southern land.
The Lord caused the snakes to stop chasing the animals but to block the path so that no one could pass through without being killed by the poisonous snakes.

The people followed the trail of the animals and ate the bodies of those that had died along the way until they had eaten them all. When the people realized they were about to die, they began to repent and cry out to the Lord.

When they had humbled themselves enough before the Lord, he sent rain upon the land. The people began to recover, and fruit began to grow in the northern regions and all the surrounding areas. The Lord showed his power by saving them from the famine.

10.text: Shez, who was a descendant of Heth (for Heth had died in the famine along with all his household except Shez), began to rebuild the broken nation.

Shez remembered the destruction of his ancestors, and he built up a righteous kingdom. He remembered what the Lord had done in bringing Jared and his brother across the ocean, and he followed the ways of the Lord. He had sons and daughters.
His oldest son, also named Shez, rebelled against him. But the younger Shez was killed by a robber because of his great wealth, which brought peace back to his father.

His father built many cities throughout the land, and the people spread across the land again. Shez lived to a very old age and had a son named Riplakish. When Shez died, Riplakish ruled in his place.

Riplakish did not do what was right before the Lord. He had many wives and concubines, and he placed heavy burdens on the people. He taxed them heavily, and with those taxes he built many elaborate buildings.

He built himself a very beautiful throne. He also built many prisons. Anyone who would not pay taxes was thrown into prison, and anyone who could not afford to pay was also imprisoned. He forced the prisoners to work constantly, and anyone who refused to work was put to death.
This is how he got all his fine goods—he even had gold refined in prison. All kinds of skilled work was done by prisoners. He afflicted the people with his immoral and wicked behavior.
After he had ruled for forty-two years, the people rebelled against him. War broke out in the land, and Riplakish was killed. His descendants were driven out of the land.

After many years, Morianton (a descendant of Riplakish) gathered an army of outcasts and attacked the people. He gained control over many cities, and the war was fierce and lasted many years. Eventually he gained power over the entire land and made himself king.
After he had established himself as king, he lightened the burden on the people, which gained him their favor. They anointed him as their king.
He treated the people fairly, but he was not righteous in his own life because of his many immoral acts. Because of this, he was cut off from the presence of the Lord.
Morianton built many cities, and the people became very wealthy during his reign—in buildings, gold, silver, grain, flocks, herds, and everything that had been restored to them.

Morianton lived to a very old age, and then he had a son named Kim. Kim ruled in his father's place for eight years after his father died. Kim did not rule in righteousness and was not favored by the Lord.

Kim's brother rebelled against him and took him captive. Kim remained a prisoner for the rest of his life. He had sons and daughters while in captivity, and in his old age he had a son named Levi. Then Kim died.
Levi served in captivity for forty-two years after his father died. Then he went to war against the king and won the kingdom for himself.

After gaining the kingdom, Levi did what was right before the Lord. The people prospered in the land. He lived to a good old age and had sons and daughters. He also had a son named Corom, whom he anointed as king in his place.
Corom did what was good before the Lord throughout his life. He had many sons and daughters. After living many years, he passed away like all people do, and Kish ruled in his place.
Kish also passed away, and Lib ruled in his place.

Lib also did what was good before the Lord. In his time the poisonous snakes were destroyed. So the people went into the southern land to hunt food, because the land was full of forest animals. Lib himself became a great hunter.
They built a great city by the narrow neck of land, near the place where the sea divides the land.
They kept the southern land as a wilderness for hunting. The entire northern land was filled with people.
They were very hardworking. They bought, sold, and traded with one another to make a profit.

They worked with all kinds of metal ore. They made things from gold, silver, iron, brass, and all kinds of metals. They dug ore out of the earth, creating huge mounds of dirt to mine gold, silver, iron, and copper. They produced all kinds of fine craftsmanship.
They had silks and fine-twined linen. They made all kinds of cloth to clothe themselves.
They made all kinds of tools for farming—for plowing, sowing, reaping, hoeing, and threshing.
They made all kinds of tools for working with their animals.
They made all kinds of weapons of war. They crafted all kinds of remarkably skilled work.
No people could have been more blessed or more prospered by the Lord. They were in a land that was the most favored of all lands, for the Lord had said so.
Lib lived many years and had sons and daughters. He also had a son named Hearthom.
Hearthom ruled in his father's place. After Hearthom had ruled for twenty-four years, the kingdom was taken from him. He spent the rest of his life as a captive.

He had a son named Heth, and Heth lived in captivity all his life. Heth had a son named Aaron, who also lived in captivity all his life. Aaron had a son named Amnigaddah, who also lived in captivity all his life. Amnigaddah had a son named Coriantum, who also lived in captivity all his life. Coriantum had a son named Com.

Com gained control of half the kingdom. He ruled over his half for forty-two years. He went to war against King Amgid, and they fought for many years until Com gained power over Amgid and took control of the rest of the kingdom.
In the days of Com, robbers appeared in the land. They adopted the old secret plans and swore oaths just like the ancients had done, seeking again to destroy the kingdom.
Com fought hard against them, but he could not defeat them.

11.text: In the days of Com, many prophets also came and warned that the people would be destroyed unless they repented, turned to the Lord, and gave up their murders and wickedness.
The people rejected the prophets, and the prophets fled to Com for protection because the people wanted to kill them.
The prophets told Com many things, and he was blessed for the rest of his life.
Com lived to a good old age and had a son named Shiblom. Shiblom ruled in his place. But Shiblom's brother rebelled against him, and a very great war broke out across the land.
Shiblom's brother had all the prophets who warned of the people's destruction put to death.

Great disaster struck the whole land, for the prophets had warned that a terrible curse would come upon the land and the people—a destruction greater than anything ever seen on earth—and that their bones would pile up like mounds of earth unless they repented of their wickedness.

But the people would not listen to the Lord because of their wicked alliances. Wars, conflicts, famines, and diseases broke out everywhere, causing destruction on a scale never before known on earth. All of this happened during the days of Shiblom.
The people began to repent of their sins, and as they did, the Lord had mercy on them.
Shiblom was killed, and Seth was taken captive and remained a prisoner for the rest of his life.
Seth's son Ahah gained the kingdom and ruled all his days. He did all kinds of evil, causing much bloodshed, and his life was short.
Ethem, a descendant of Ahah, gained the kingdom. He also did what was wicked.
In Ethem's time, many prophets came again and warned the people that the Lord would completely destroy them from the earth unless they repented of their sins.
But the people hardened their hearts and would not listen. The prophets mourned and withdrew from the people.
Ethem ruled wickedly all his life. He had a son named Moron, who ruled in his place. Moron also did what was wicked before the Lord.

A rebellion arose among the people because of the secret alliance that had been built to gain power and wealth. A mighty but wicked man rose up and fought against Moron, taking half the kingdom from him. He held that half of the kingdom for many years.
Moron eventually overthrew him and took the kingdom back.
Then another mighty man arose. He was a descendant of the brother of Jared.
He overthrew Moron and took the kingdom. Moron lived in captivity for the rest of his life. He had a son named Coriantor.
Coriantor also lived in captivity all his days.

In Coriantor's time, many prophets came and prophesied great and marvelous things. They called the people to repentance, warning that unless they repented, the Lord God would bring total destruction upon them.
They also said that the Lord God would bring another people to possess the land, by his power, just as he had brought their ancestors.
But the people rejected everything the prophets said because of their secret society and wicked practices.
Coriantor had a son named Ether, and then he died, having lived in captivity all his life.

12.text: Ether lived in the days of Coriantumr, and Coriantumr was king over the entire land.
Ether was a prophet of the Lord. He came forward during the days of Coriantumr and began to prophesy to the people, for he could not hold back because of the Spirit of the Lord that was in him.
He cried out from morning until sunset, urging the people to believe in God and repent so they would not be destroyed. He told them that through faith all things are fulfilled—

Therefore, whoever believes in God can confidently hope for a better world—a place at the right hand of God. This hope comes from faith and serves as an anchor to the soul, making people sure and steadfast, always doing good works, and being led to glorify God.
Ether prophesied great and marvelous things to the people, but they did not believe him because they could not see those things.

Now I, Moroni, want to say something about this. I want to show the world that faith is about things we hope for but cannot see. So do not argue just because you cannot see, for you receive no witness until after your faith has been tested.

It was by faith that Christ showed himself to our ancestors after he had risen from the dead. He did not show himself to them until after they had faith in him. So there had to be some who believed in him first, because he did not show himself to the whole world.

But because of people's faith, he has shown himself to the world and glorified the name of the Father. He prepared a way so that others could share in the heavenly gift and hope for things they have not seen.
Therefore, you too can have hope and share in the gift, if you will just have faith.
It was by faith that people in ancient times were called to the holy order of God.
It was by faith that the law of Moses was given. But through the gift of his Son, God prepared a more excellent way, and it is by faith that it has been fulfilled.
For if there is no faith among the people, God cannot perform any miracles among them. That is why he did not show himself until after they had faith.
It was the faith of Alma and Amulek that caused the prison walls to collapse to the ground.
It was the faith of Nephi and Lehi that brought about the change in the Lamanites, so that they were baptized with fire and with the Holy Ghost.
It was the faith of Ammon and his brothers that worked such a great miracle among the Lamanites.
Yes, everyone who performed miracles did so by faith—both those who lived before Christ and those who came after.
It was by faith that the three disciples received a promise that they would not die. They did not receive that promise until after they had shown their faith.
No one has ever performed miracles without first having faith. They first had to believe in the Son of God.

There were many whose faith was so incredibly strong, even before Christ came, that they could not be kept from looking beyond the veil. They truly saw with their own eyes the things they had seen through the eye of faith, and they rejoiced.

We have seen in this record that one of these was the brother of Jared. His faith in God was so great that when God put forth his finger, he could not hide it from the brother of Jared—because of the promise he had spoken to him, which promise he had received through faith.

After the brother of Jared had seen the finger of the Lord, because of the promise he had received through faith, the Lord could not keep anything hidden from him. So the Lord showed him all things, for he could no longer be kept outside the veil.
It is by faith that my ancestors received the promise that these things would come to their descendants through the Gentiles. Therefore the Lord has commanded me—yes, even Jesus Christ himself.

And I said to him: Lord, the Gentiles will mock at these things because of our weakness in writing. Lord, you have made us powerful in speaking by faith, but you have not made us powerful in writing. You made all these people able to speak with great skill because of the Holy Ghost you gave them.

But you have made us so that we can only write a little, because our hands are clumsy at it. You did not make us mighty in writing like the brother of Jared, for you caused the things he wrote to be as powerful as you are—so powerful they overwhelm anyone who reads them.

You have also made our spoken words powerful and great—so much so that we cannot capture them in writing. When we write, we see our weakness and stumble over how to arrange the words. I am afraid the Gentiles will mock at our words.
When I had said this, the Lord spoke to me and said: Fools will mock, but they will mourn. My grace is sufficient for the humble, and they will not take advantage of your weakness.

And if people come to me, I will show them their weakness. I give people weakness so that they may be humble. My grace is sufficient for all who humble themselves before me. For if they humble themselves before me and have faith in me, then I will make weak things become strong for them.
I will show the Gentiles their weakness, and I will show them that faith, hope, and charity lead them to me—the fountain of all righteousness.
I, Moroni, was comforted when I heard these words, and I said: O Lord, your righteous will be done, for I know that you work with people according to their faith.
For the brother of Jared said to the mountain Zerin, “Be removed”—and it was removed. If he had not had faith, it would not have moved. You work with people only after they have faith.
This is how you showed yourself to your disciples—after they had faith and spoke in your name, you showed yourself to them in great power.

I also remember that you said you have prepared a home for people—among the mansions of your Father—where they can have a greater hope. Therefore people must hope, or they cannot receive an inheritance in the place you have prepared.
And again, I remember that you said you have loved the world so much that you laid down your life for it, so that you could take it up again and prepare a place for all people.
Now I know that this love you have for all people is charity. Unless people have charity, they cannot inherit the place you have prepared in the mansions of your Father.

Therefore I know from what you have said that if the Gentiles do not have charity because of our weakness, you will test them and take away their gifts—even what they have received—and give to those who have more abundantly.
So I prayed to the Lord that he would give the Gentiles grace, so that they might have charity.

And the Lord said to me: If they do not have charity, it does not matter for you—you have been faithful. Your garments will be made clean. And because you have recognized your weakness, you will be made strong—even to the point of sitting in the place I have prepared in the mansions of my Father.

Now I, Moroni, say farewell to the Gentiles, and also to my beloved brothers and sisters, until we meet before the judgment seat of Christ, where everyone will know that my garments are not stained with your blood.

Then you will know that I have seen Jesus, and that he has spoken with me face to face, and that he told me in plain and simple terms, just as one person speaks to another in my own language, about these things.
I have only written a few of these things because of my weakness in writing.

And now I urge you to seek this Jesus, about whom the prophets and apostles have written, so that the grace of God the Father, and of the Lord Jesus Christ, and of the Holy Ghost, who testifies of them, may be with you and remain in you forever. Amen.

13.text: Now I, Moroni, continue to finish my record about the destruction of the people I have been writing about.

The people rejected everything Ether said. He truly told them about all things from the beginning of humanity—that after the floodwaters had gone down from this land, it became the most favored land of all, a chosen land of the Lord. The Lord wanted everyone living here to serve him.
He taught that this was the place of the New Jerusalem, which would come down from heaven, and the holy sanctuary of the Lord.
Ether saw the days of Christ, and he spoke about a New Jerusalem on this land.

He also spoke about the house of Israel and the Jerusalem that Lehi would come from. After it was destroyed, it would be built up again as a holy city for the Lord. It could not be called a new Jerusalem because it had existed long ago, but it would be rebuilt and become a holy city of the Lord, built for the house of Israel.
A New Jerusalem would be built on this land for the descendants of Joseph, as a fulfillment of what had been foreshadowed.

Just as Joseph brought his father down into Egypt and died there, the Lord brought a remnant of Joseph's descendants out of Jerusalem so he could be merciful to them and keep them from being destroyed—just as he was merciful to Joseph's father so he would not be destroyed.

Therefore, the remnant of the house of Joseph will be established on this land. It will be their land of inheritance, and they will build a holy city for the Lord, like the Jerusalem of old. They will never again be overthrown, until the end comes when the earth passes away.
There will be a new heaven and a new earth, and they will be like the old ones, except the old will have passed away and all things will have become new.

Then the New Jerusalem will come. Blessed are those who live there, for their garments are white through the blood of the Lamb. They are numbered among the descendants of Joseph, who were of the house of Israel.

Then also the old Jerusalem will come. Blessed are its people, for they have been washed in the blood of the Lamb. They are those who were scattered and gathered from the four corners of the earth and from the northern lands. They share in the fulfillment of the covenant God made with their father Abraham.
When these things happen, the scripture will be fulfilled that says: Those who were first will be last, and those who were last will be first.

I was about to write more, but I was told not to. The prophecies of Ether were great and marvelous, but the people considered him worthless and threw him out. He hid in a cave during the day, and at night he went out to see the things that would happen to the people.
While living in the cave, he wrote the rest of this record, watching the destruction that came upon the people by night.

In the same year he was cast out, a great war began among the people. Many powerful men rose up and tried to destroy Coriantumr through their secret plots of wickedness, as has already been described.
Now Coriantumr had studied all the arts of war and all the cleverness of the world, so he fought back against those who tried to destroy him.

But he did not repent, nor did his handsome sons and daughters, nor the handsome sons and daughters of Cohor, nor those of Corihor. In fact, none of the fine sons and daughters anywhere on the earth repented of their sins.
In the first year that Ether lived in the cave, many people were killed by the swords of those secret alliances that were fighting against Coriantumr to seize the kingdom.
The sons of Coriantumr fought fiercely and suffered many wounds.

In the second year, the word of the Lord came to Ether, telling him to go and prophesy to Coriantumr that if he and all his household would repent, the Lord would give him his kingdom and spare the people.

Otherwise, they would all be destroyed—everyone in his household except Coriantumr himself. He alone would survive to see the prophecies fulfilled about another people receiving the land as their inheritance. Coriantumr would be buried by them, and every other person would be destroyed except Coriantumr.
But Coriantumr did not repent, nor did his household, nor the people. The wars continued, and they tried to kill Ether, but he fled and hid again in the cave.
A man named Shared rose up and fought against Coriantumr. He defeated him and took him captive in the third year.
In the fourth year, the sons of Coriantumr defeated Shared and restored the kingdom to their father.
War had now spread across the entire land, with every man and his followers fighting for whatever they wanted.
There were robbers and every kind of wickedness throughout the land.
Coriantumr was extremely angry with Shared and went against him with his armies. They met in fierce anger in the valley of Gilgal, and the battle was very intense.
Shared fought against him for three days. Then Coriantumr defeated him and chased him to the plains of Heshlon.
Shared fought him again on the plains. This time Shared defeated Coriantumr and drove him back to the valley of Gilgal.
Coriantumr fought Shared again in the valley of Gilgal, and this time he defeated and killed Shared.

But Shared wounded Coriantumr in his thigh, so badly that he could not go to battle for two years. During that time, all the people throughout the land continued shedding blood, and no one could stop them.

14.text: A great curse came upon the entire land because of the people's wickedness. If a man set down his tool or sword on a shelf or wherever he kept it, the next morning he could not find it—so severe was the curse on the land.
Because of this, every man held tightly to his own belongings and would not borrow or lend. Every man kept his sword in his right hand to defend his property, his own life, and his wife and children.
After two years, and after Shared's death, the brother of Shared rose up and fought against Coriantumr. Coriantumr defeated him and chased him to the wilderness of Akish.
The brother of Shared fought him in the wilderness of Akish. The battle was extremely fierce, and many thousands died by the sword.
Coriantumr laid siege to the wilderness. The brother of Shared marched out of the wilderness at night and killed part of Coriantumr's army while they were drunk.
He then went to the land of Moron and placed himself on Coriantumr's throne.
Coriantumr stayed in the wilderness with his army for two years, during which time his army grew much stronger.
The brother of Shared, whose name was Gilead, also strengthened his army greatly because of secret alliances.
Gilead's own high priest murdered him while he sat on his throne.
One of the secret alliance members murdered him in a hidden passage and took the kingdom for himself. His name was Lib, and he was a man of great height—taller than any other person among the people.
In the first year of Lib's reign, Coriantumr came up to the land of Moron and fought against Lib.
In the battle, Lib struck Coriantumr's arm and wounded him. But Coriantumr's army pressed forward against Lib, and Lib fled to the borders by the seashore.
Coriantumr chased him, and Lib fought him at the seashore.
Lib defeated Coriantumr's army, and they fled back to the wilderness of Akish.
Lib chased him until he reached the plains of Agosh. Coriantumr had taken all the people with him as he fled from Lib through that part of the land.

When he reached the plains of Agosh, he fought Lib and struck him until Lib died. But Lib's brother then came against Coriantumr, and the battle became extremely fierce. Coriantumr fled again before the army of Lib's brother.
The name of Lib's brother was Shiz. Shiz chased after Coriantumr and overthrew many cities. He killed both women and children and burned the cities.
Fear of Shiz spread throughout the entire land. A cry went throughout the land: “Who can stand against the army of Shiz? He sweeps the earth before him!”
People began gathering into armies throughout the land.
They were divided—some fled to Shiz's army, and some fled to Coriantumr's army.
The war had been so great and so long, and the bloodshed and slaughter so extensive, that the entire land was covered with dead bodies.

The war moved so quickly that there was no one left to bury the dead. They marched from one scene of bloodshed to another, leaving the bodies of men, women, and children scattered across the land to be eaten by worms.
The stench spread across the entire land, and the people were troubled day and night because of it.
Still, Shiz would not stop pursuing Coriantumr, because he had sworn to avenge the death of his brother. And the word of the Lord had come to Ether that Coriantumr would not die by the sword.
And so we see that the Lord visited them with the fullness of his anger, and their wickedness and terrible sins had paved the way for their everlasting destruction.
Shiz chased Coriantumr eastward to the borders by the seashore, where Coriantumr fought Shiz for three days.

The destruction among Shiz's armies was so terrible that the people became frightened and began to flee from Coriantumr's armies. They fled to the land of Corihor, driving out everyone who would not join them.

They set up camp in the valley of Corihor, and Coriantumr set up camp in the valley of Shurr. The valley of Shurr was near the hill Comnor, so Coriantumr gathered his armies on the hill Comnor and sounded a trumpet to challenge Shiz's armies to battle.
They came to fight but were driven back. They came a second time and were driven back again. They came a third time, and the battle became extremely fierce.
Shiz struck Coriantumr and gave him many deep wounds. Coriantumr lost so much blood that he fainted and was carried away as if he were dead.
The loss of men, women, and children on both sides was so great that Shiz ordered his people not to pursue Coriantumr's armies. So they returned to their camp.

15.text: When Coriantumr had recovered from his wounds, he began to remember the words that Ether had spoken to him.
He saw that nearly two million of his people had already been killed by the sword, and he began to grieve deeply. Two million mighty men had been killed, along with their wives and children.

He began to repent of the evil he had done. He remembered the words spoken by all the prophets, and he saw that every one of them had been fulfilled so far. His soul mourned and could not be comforted.
He wrote a letter to Shiz, asking him to spare the people, and offering to give up the kingdom in exchange for the people's lives.
When Shiz received the letter, he wrote back to Coriantumr saying he would spare the people only if Coriantumr would surrender himself so Shiz could kill him with his own sword.

The people did not repent of their sins. Coriantumr's people were stirred up to anger against Shiz's people, and Shiz's people were stirred up to anger against Coriantumr's people. So Shiz's people attacked Coriantumr's people.
When Coriantumr saw he was about to be defeated, he fled from Shiz's people.
He came to the waters of Ripliancum, which means “large, or surpassing all.” When they reached these waters, they set up camp, and Shiz also set up camp nearby. The next day they went to battle.
They fought a terribly fierce battle. Coriantumr was wounded again and fainted from the loss of blood.
Coriantumr's armies pushed back Shiz's armies, causing them to flee. They fled southward and set up camp at a place called Ogath.
Coriantumr's army set up camp by the hill Ramah. This was the same hill where my father Mormon hid the sacred records for the Lord.
They gathered together all the people in the entire land who had not been killed—everyone except Ether.
Ether watched everything the people did. He saw that the people who supported Coriantumr gathered to Coriantumr's army, and the people who supported Shiz gathered to Shiz's army.
They spent four years gathering people so they could get every person in the land and gain all the strength they possibly could.

When they were all gathered together, each person to whichever army they chose—men, women, and children all armed with weapons of war, carrying shields, breastplates, and helmets, and dressed for battle—they marched against each other. They fought all that day but neither side won.

When night came, they were exhausted and went back to their camps. After returning, they howled and cried out in grief for all their people who had been killed. Their cries and mourning were so loud they filled the air.

The next day they went to battle again, and that day was terrible. But neither side won, and when night came, they again filled the air with their cries, howling, and mourning for those who had been killed.
Coriantumr wrote another letter to Shiz, asking him not to come to battle again. He offered to give up the kingdom if Shiz would spare the people's lives.

But the Spirit of the Lord had stopped working with them, and Satan had full power over the people's hearts. They had given themselves over to hard hearts and blind minds so that they could be destroyed. So they went to battle again.
They fought all that day, and when night came, they slept on their swords.
The next day they fought again until nightfall.
When night came, they were overwhelmed with rage, like people drunk with wine. They slept on their swords again.
The next morning they fought again, and by nightfall everyone had been killed except fifty-two of Coriantumr's people and sixty-nine of Shiz's people.
They slept on their swords that night. The next day they fought again, battling with all their strength, using swords and shields, all day long.
When night came, there were only thirty-two of Shiz's people left and twenty-seven of Coriantumr's people.
They ate, slept, and prepared to die the next day. They were large and powerful men.
They fought for three hours, then fainted from the loss of blood.

When Coriantumr's men had gained enough strength to walk, they were about to flee for their lives. But Shiz got up, along with his men, and swore in his rage that he would kill Coriantumr or die by the sword.
So he chased them, and the next day he caught up to them. They fought again with swords. When everyone had fallen except Coriantumr and Shiz, Shiz had fainted from the loss of blood.
Coriantumr leaned on his sword to rest for a moment, then cut off the head of Shiz.
After Coriantumr had cut off his head, Shiz rose up on his hands and fell. After struggling for breath, he died.
Coriantumr fell to the ground and lay as if he had no life.

The Lord spoke to Ether and said: Go out. Ether went out and saw that every word of the Lord had been fulfilled. He finished his record (and I have not written even a hundredth part of it) and hid the plates in a way that the people of Limhi later found them.

The last words Ether wrote are these: Whether the Lord wants me to be translated or to endure whatever the Lord wills while in the flesh, it does not matter, as long as I am saved in the kingdom of God. Amen.
